summary: The subscapularis, supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor attach or support attachment at the greater tubercle but only one option lacks this specific muscular seeding; thus, Teres Major stands out for its absence from these attachments. Agent1 identified which muscle among those listed did not have an origin point specifically connected with the greater tuberosity based purely upon known anatomy where other specified muscles do indeed originate here. This led them confidently concluding 'Teres Major' was correct given no explicit description linked it to such location despite being part of broader upper limb musculature supporting overall joint function indirectly through different mechanisms like stabilization rather than primary movement generation roles typical elsewhere within mentioned groupings.
